methods.name: breakCurve
methods.description: Breaks a curve into two or three pieces by finding intersections of this curve with all other curves in the sketch and splitting this curve at the nearest intersections to a specified point on the curve.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the entity from the sketch.

methods.name: extend
methods.description: Extend a curve by specifying a point that determines the end of the curve to extend

methods.name: intersections
methods.description: Get the curves that intersect this curve along with the intersection points (Point3D)

methods.name: split
methods.description: Split a curve at a position specified along the curve

methods.name: trim
methods.description: Trim a curve by specifying a point that determines the segment of the curve to trim away

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this face.

properties.name: boundingBox
properties.description: Returns the bounding box of the entity in sketch space.

properties.name: centerSketchPoint
properties.description: The sketch point at the center of the arc. The arc is dependent on this point and moving the point will cause the arc to adjust.

properties.name: endSketchPoint
properties.description: The sketch point at the end of the arc. The arc is dependent on this point and moving the point will cause the arc to adjust.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the SketchEntity object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same sketch entity.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: geometricConstraints
properties.description: Returns the sketch constraints that are attached to this curve.

properties.name: geometry
properties.description: Returns the transient geometry of the arc which provides geometric information about the arc. The returned geometry is always in sketch space.

properties.name: is2D
properties.description: Indicates if this curve lies entirely on the sketch x-y plane.

properties.name: isConstruction
properties.description: Gets and sets whether this curve is construction geometry.

properties.name: isDeletable
properties.description: Indicates if this sketch entity can be deleted. There are cases, especially with sketch points where another entity is dependent on an entity so deleting it is not allowed. For example, you can't delete the center point of circle by itself but deleting the circle will delete the point. The same is true for the end points of a line.

properties.name: isFixed
properties.description: Indicates if this geometry is "fixed".

properties.name: isFullyConstrained
properties.description: Indicates if this sketch entity is fully constrained.

properties.name: isLinked
properties.description: Indicates if this sketch entity was created by a projection, inclusion, or driven by an API script. If this returns true, then the entity is presented to the user as not editable and with a 'break link' command available.

properties.name: isReference
properties.description: Indicates if this geometry is a reference. Changing this property from true to false removes the reference. This property can not be set to true if it is already false.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: When a sketch is created, geometry is sometimes automatically added to the sketch. For example a sketch point that references the origin point is always included and if a face was selected to create the sketch on, geometry from the face is also included. This automatically created geometry behaves in a special way in that it is invisible but is available for selection and it also participates in profile calculations. It's not possible to make them visible but they can be deleted and they can be used for any other standard sketch operation.

properties.name: length
properties.description: Returns the length of the curve in centimeters.

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly and in the context of it's parent component. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentSketch
properties.description: Returns the parent sketch.

properties.name: radius
properties.description: Gets and sets the radius of the arc. Changing the radius is limited by any constraints that might exist on the circle. Setting the radius can fail in cases where the radius is fully defined through constraints.

properties.name: referencedEntity
properties.description: Returns the referenced entity in the case where IsReference is true. However, this property can also return null when IsReference is true in the case where the reference is not parametric.

properties.name: sketchDimensions
properties.description: Returns the sketch dimensions that are attached to this curve.

properties.name: startSketchPoint
properties.description: The sketch point at the start of the arc. The arc is dependent on this point and moving the point will cause the arc to adjust.

properties.name: worldGeometry
properties.description: Returns an Arc3D object which provides geometric information in world space. The returned geometry takes into account the assembly context and the position of the sketch in it's parent component, which means the geometry will be returned in the root component space.

assemblyContext.title: SketchArc.assemblyContext Property
assemblyContext.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchArc_var.assemblyContext

entityToken.title: SketchArc.entityToken Property
entityToken.description: Returns a token for the SketchEntity object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same sketch entity. When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

breakCurve.title: SketchArc.breakCurve Method
breakCurve.description: Breaks a curve into two or three pieces by finding intersections of this curve with all other curves in the sketch and splitting this curve at the nearest intersections to a specified point on the curve.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArc_var.breakCurve(segmentPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArc_var.breakCurve(segmentPoint, createConstraints)

breakCurve.return_description: All of the curves resulting from the break are returned in an ObjectCollection. In the case where no intersections are found and as a result the curve is not broken, an empty ObjectCollection is returned.

parameters.name: segmentPoint
parameters.description: A point that specifies the segment of the curve that is to be split from the rest of the curve. The nearest intersection(s) to this point define the break location(s).

parameters.name: createConstraints
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies if constraints should be created between the new curve segments. A value of true indicates constraints will be created.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

classType.title: SketchArc.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.SketchArc.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::SketchArc::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createForAssemblyContext.title: SketchArc.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchArc_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchArc_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy object or null if this isn't the NativeObject.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that defines the context to create the proxy in.

deleteMe.title: SketchArc.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the entity from the sketch.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchArc_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchArc_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns true is the delete was successful.

extend.title: SketchArc.extend Method
extend.description: Extend a curve by specifying a point that determines the end of the curve to extend

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArc_var.extend(endPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArc_var.extend(endPoint, createConstraints)

extend.return_description: Returns the modified original curve if the start or end of the curve is extended If the extend joins a curve to another, the two original curves are deleted and a new curve is returned If an arc is extended so as to become a circle, the original arc is deleted and a new circle is returned

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: A point (transient Point3D) on or closest to the end of the curve to extend. (start or end) The end of the curve closest to the endPoint gets extended

parameters.name: createConstraints
parameters.description: Constraints are created by default. Specify false to not create constraints.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

intersections.title: SketchArc.intersections Method
intersections.description: Get the curves that intersect this curve along with the intersection points (Point3D)

syntax.python: (returnValue, intersectingCurves, intersectionPoints) = sketchArc_var.intersections(sketchCurves)

intersections.return_description: Returns true if the method was successful. It can be successful regardless of whether intersections were found or not.

parameters.name: sketchCurves
parameters.description: A collection of curves to attempt to find intersections with. Set the value of this parameter to null to use all curves in the sketch for the calculation.

parameters.name: intersectingCurves
parameters.description: A collection of the actual intersecting curves

parameters.name: intersectionPoints
parameters.description: A collection of intersection points (Point3D) Item numbers in this collection correspond to the item numbers in the intersectingCurves collection.

split.title: SketchArc.split Method
split.description: Split a curve at a position specified along the curve

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArc_var.split(splitPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArc_var.split(splitPoint, createConstraints)

split.return_description: Returns the resulting 2 curves; the original curve + the newly created curve When split spline the original is deleted and two new curves returned. Empty collection returned if curve is closed.

parameters.name: splitPoint
parameters.description: A position (transient Point3D) on the curve that defines the point at which to split the curve

parameters.name: createConstraints
parameters.description: Constraints are created by default. Specify false to create no constraints.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

trim.title: SketchArc.trim Method
trim.description: Trim a curve by specifying a point that determines the segment of the curve to trim away

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArc_var.trim(segmentPoint)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = sketchArc_var.trim(segmentPoint, createConstraints)

trim.return_description: When trimming the start or end side of a line, unclosed circular or elliptical arc, the original entity is modified and returned When trimming the middle of a line, unclosed circular or elliptical arc the original entity is deleted and two new entities are returned When trimming the start or end of any type of closed curve, the original is deleted and a new curve is returned Any trimming of a spline (open or closed) deletes the original and new spline/s are returned Trimming a curve having no intersections deletes the original and returns an empty collection

parameters.name: segmentPoint
parameters.description: A point (transient Point3D) on or closest to the segment of the curve to remove. (start, end or middle) The segment of the curve closest to the segmentPoint gets removed

parameters.name: createConstraints
parameters.description: Constraints are created by default. Specify false to not create constraints.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.
